title: Observability, SLOs, Drift, Incidents, and Recovery · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers observability, slos, drift, incidents, and recovery into a new redacted stage trace and reliability dashboard. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of redacted stage trace and reliability dashboard with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Trace retrieval and generation operations with stable run, corpus, model, index, query, document, score, error, and version attributes aligned to current GenAI conventions.
Define availability, freshness, candidate recall proxy, grounded-answer success, citation integrity, latency, saturation, and cost service indicators and objectives.
Keep query and content capture disabled or tightly governed and bound labels, exemplars, hashes, sampling, retention, and cardinality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Stage Tracing Semantics
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 1059 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to trace every RAG stage with bounded redacted signals, SLOs, causal faults, and rollback readiness; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Incident Causal Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-incident-causal-recovery-1bo5g8" that uses case 1881 to trace every RAG stage with bounded redacted signals, SLOs, causal faults, and rollback readiness. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Incident Causal Recovery.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-incident-causal-recovery-1bo5g8
# Competency: rag-incident-causal-recovery.
# Case 1881: an enterprise retrieval migration canary.
# Operating constraint: keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_incident_causal_recovery_1bo5g8(trace_stages: int = 6, expected_stages: int = 6, retrieval_p95_ms: float = 42.0, error_rate: float = 0.01, content_logged: bool = False, rollback_ready: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bo5g8 = "1bo5g8-8g5ob1-1881"
    assert evidence_variant_1bo5g8
    evidence_axes_1bo5g8 = (
        "corpus1bo5g8",
        "query8g5ob1",
        "tenant1881",
        "index1bo5g81881",
        "prompt8g5ob11881",
        "evaluation18811bo5g8",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bo5g8)) == 6
    if trace_stages != expected_stages or expected_stages < 5:
        return False, trace_stages, "trace-completeness"
    if retrieval_p95_ms <= 0.0 or error_rate < 0.0 or error_rate > 1.0:
        return False, error_rate, "service-signal"
    if content_logged:
        return False, error_rate, "sensitive-telemetry"
    if not rollback_ready:
        return False, error_rate, "recovery-readiness"
    assert trace_stages == expected_stages
    return True, round(retrieval_p95_ms, 2), "redacted-stage-trace"
